result:
## CONFIG_SAE-dependent functions in `src/`

**Method:** `grep -rn CONFIG_SAE src/` found 10 files with `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` (all instances checked, no `defined(CONFIG_SAE)` variants found). For each hit I located the enclosing function via `LSP documentSymbol`/`goToDefinition` and read the surrounding code to see whether the `#ifdef` wraps the whole function or just part of its body. I also checked `wpa_supplicant/Makefile`, which shows `src/common/sae.o` is only added to `OBJS` `ifdef CONFIG_SAE` — so **every function in `src/common/sae.c`** is build-gated at the file level even though the file itself contains no internal `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE`.

### Whole-file gating: `src/common/sae.c` (35 functions, all "whole")
Compiled only when `CONFIG_SAE` is defined (per `wpa_supplicant/Makefile` `OBJS += ../src/common/sae.o` inside `ifdef CONFIG_SAE`):
`sae_set_group`, `sae_clear_temp_data`, `sae_clear_data`, `buf_shift_right`, `sae_get_rand`, `sae_get_rand_and_mask`, `sae_pwd_seed_key`, `get_rand_1_to_p_1`, `is_quadratic_residue_blind`, `sae_test_pwd_seed_ecc`, `sae_test_pwd_seed_ffc`, `get_random_qr_qnr`, `sae_derive_pwe_ecc`, `sae_derive_pwe_ffc`, `sae_derive_commit_element_ecc`, `sae_derive_commit_element_ffc`, `sae_derive_commit`, `sae_prepare_commit`, `sae_derive_k_ecc`, `sae_derive_k_ffc`, `sae_derive_keys`, `sae_process_commit`, `sae_write_commit`, `sae_group_allowed`, `sae_parse_commit_token`, `sae_parse_commit_scalar`, `sae_parse_commit_element_ecc`, `sae_parse_commit_element_ffc`, `sae_parse_commit_element`, `sae_parse_commit`, `sae_cn_confirm`, `sae_cn_confirm_ecc`, `sae_cn_confirm_ffc`, `sae_write_confirm`, `sae_check_confirm`.

### `src/ap/ieee802_11.c` — `#ifdef` block at lines 318–876 (WHOLE, 14 functions)
Entirely inside a single `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE … #endif`:
`auth_build_sae_commit` (323), `auth_build_sae_confirm` (352), `auth_sae_send_commit` (367), `auth_sae_send_confirm` (387), `use_sae_anti_clogging` (407), `check_sae_token` (430), `auth_build_token_req` (446), `sae_check_big_sync` (478), `auth_sae_retransmit_timer` (489), `sae_clear_retransmit_timer` (522), `sae_set_retransmit_timer` (528), `sae_sm_step` (540), `handle_auth_sae` (697), `auth_sae_init_committed` (855).

*Special case — `sae_clear_retransmit_timer`*: declared twice in `src/ap/ieee802_11.h` (97–105): the real prototype under `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE`, and a `static inline` no-op stub in the `#else`. So the name always exists, but the real (working) function body only exists under `CONFIG_SAE` — classified "whole".

### `src/ap/ieee802_11.c` — 3 more blocks (PARTIAL, 2 functions)
- `handle_auth` (def. line 879): two separate `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` regions inside it — 954–957 (adds `WLAN_AUTH_SAE` to the accepted-auth-algorithm check) and 1180–1200 (the `case WLAN_AUTH_SAE:` branch of the auth-transaction switch).
- `check_assoc_ies` (def. line 1334): block at 1538–1560 (SAE PMKSA-caching / auth_alg validation logic).

### Other files (all PARTIAL — function exists regardless of CONFIG_SAE, only part of the body is conditional)

| File | Function | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `src/ap/ctrl_iface_ap.c` | `hostapd_ctrl_iface_sta_mib` | 148–155 |
| `src/ap/sta_info.c` | `ap_free_sta` | 313–316 |
| `src/ap/wpa_auth_glue.c` | `hostapd_wpa_auth_get_psk` | 231–237 |
| `src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c` | `wpa_write_rsn_ie` | 191–202 |
| `src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c` | `wpa_validate_wpa_ie` | 524–529 **and** 606–611 (two separate blocks, same function) |
| `src/common/wpa_common.c` | `rsn_key_mgmt_to_bitfield` | 485–490 |
| `src/rsn_supp/wpa_ie.c` | `wpa_gen_wpa_ie_rsn` | 173–178 |

That's 7 more partial functions.

### Non-function sites (mentioned for completeness, not counted as functions)
- `src/ap/hostapd.h` (268–273): `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` guards struct fields in `struct hostapd_data` (`sae_token_key`, `last_sae_token_key_update`, `dot11RSNASAERetransPeriod`) — affects struct layout/size, used by every function that touches `hostapd_data`, but is not itself a function.
- `src/ap/sta_info.h` (162–164): guards the `struct sae_data *sae;` field in `struct sta_info` — same, struct-layout only.

## Totals
- **Whole functions:** 35 (sae.c) + 14 (ieee802_11.c) = **49**
- **Partial functions:** 2 (ieee802_11.c) + 7 (other files) = **9**
- **Grand total: 58 functions** whose compiled behavior depends on `CONFIG_SAE`, across 10 files (plus 1 whole-file-gated implementation file and 2 struct-layout-only header sites).
